data.title: A whale established a $183 million long position in BTC with a "10-point target" in mind, and is currently showing a floating profit of $1.113 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale, "Setting 10 Big Targets First," established a $183 million long position in BTC, currently showing a floating profit of $1.113 million. @Jason60704294 has completed a long position of 2601.5 BTC, with a total position value of $183 million. The opening price for this trade was $70,016.98, the liquidation price was $57,855, and the current floating profit is $1.113 million.

data.title: Bedrock: uniBTC holders can obtain fixed spreads from institutional underwriting through Cap.
data.description: Bedrock stated in an article on the X platform that by leveraging Cap's credit engine, uniBTC holders can now obtain a fixed spread from institutional underwriting. This scheme expands the utility of BTC by utilizing the credit of on-chain financial institutions to provide sustainable returns.

data.title: Intuition founder billΞ.eth lost $703,000 selling 5,571 ETH.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, Intuition founder billΞ.eth (0xbilly) sold 5571 ETH in the past 10 hours at an average price of $2113.77, incurring a loss of $703,000. This same address had previously purchased 7768.56 ETH on-chain at an average price of $2240.1 on March 16th, worth $17.4 million. The remaining ETH at this address has now been deposited into EtherFi.

data.title: Hong Kong Attracts Record Number of Overseas and Mainland Enterprises
data.description:
Hong Kong's InvestHK assisted 560 overseas and mainland enterprises in establishing operations in the city last year, marking a more than 4% increase from the previous year and setting a new record. According to RTHK, these enterprises brought nearly HKD 70 billion in investments and created over 10,000 new jobs. InvestHK Assistant Director Lau Chi-yuen highlighted in an interview that the record number of companies reflects Hong Kong's attractive business environment and global investors' confidence in the city's prospects.

Lau noted that the newly established outbound task force, with its cross-bureau, cross-departmental resources and proactive approach, has been instrumental in attracting mainland enterprises to Hong Kong, especially after the U.S. introduced reciprocal tariffs last year. Mainland companies have increased their supply chain projects overseas, viewing Hong Kong as an ideal testing ground.

The government is accelerating the development of the Northern Metropolis, and Lau mentioned that potential mainland enterprises have shown significant interest in the area's development model. He believes the Northern Metropolis will become a key attraction for mainland companies considering establishing a presence in Hong Kong. InvestHK is intensifying its promotion of the Northern Metropolis, particularly its incentive packages, which include land grants, tax benefits, talent acquisition, and industry incentives, to attract more mainland enterprises to the city.

data.title: Global Companies Make Significant Advances in Cryptocurrency Adoption
data.description: Global companies have made notable progress in diversifying their financial strategies and adopting compliant custody solutions, according to ChainCatcher. Key developments include Acurx Pharmaceuticals' board approval of a $10 million Bitcoin purchase to hedge against inflation risks affecting long-term research funds. Enfusion has updated its financial guidelines to allocate 10% of its surplus cash into Bitcoin and Ethereum, aiming to enhance the risk-adjusted returns of its treasury. Nature's Miracle has initiated a $5 million Bitcoin strategic treasury plan, marking a significant step for agritech firms in accepting cryptocurrency as a reserve currency. Additionally, Bakkt's Q4 forecast indicates a 40% year-over-year increase in enterprise-level crypto asset custody, highlighting the growing reliance on compliant third-party custodians by non-native crypto companies entering the market.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Delays China Visit by Six Weeks
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on Thursday that he has postponed his visit to China by approximately six weeks. According to Ming Pao, Trump informed reporters in the Oval Office that the schedule has been rearranged and expressed anticipation for the upcoming trip.

data.title: Polymarket Bet on Spurs vs. Suns Sees Price Shift at Halftime
data.description: A Polymarket account, known for maintaining a win rate exceeding 60%, placed a $75,000 wager on the Spurs to triumph over the Suns at an initial price of 80.9¢. According to NS3.AI, by halftime, the Spurs were trailing with a score of 59:52, leading to a market price adjustment to 68¢.

data.title: New South Wales to Ban New Coal Mine Applications
data.description: New South Wales, Australia's second-largest coal-producing state, has announced a ban on new coal mine applications. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the state's commitment to transitioning towards more sustainable energy sources. This decision aligns with global efforts to reduce reliance on fossil fuels and address climate change. The move is expected to have significant implications for the coal industry in the region, which has been a major contributor to the local economy. Authorities are focusing on developing alternative energy projects to support economic growth while reducing environmental impact. The ban reflects a broader trend of shifting towards renewable energy sources in response to increasing environmental concerns.

data.title: Sources say Saudi oil officials are concerned that oil prices could rise above $180 a barrel.
data.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that Saudi oil officials are concerned oil prices could rise above $180 a barrel, sources say. Saudi Arabia expects oil prices to surge to $180 if the energy crisis continues beyond April.

data.title: A whale (a large investor) leveraged 20x to go long on BTC and ETH, with a position valued at over $40 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale has switched from short to long positions and started using 20x leverage to go long on BTC and ETH. This whale currently holds 284.68 BTC (worth $20 million) and 9336 ETH (worth $20 million), and has profited over $4 million through such frequent operations.

data.title: An account with a winning percentage over 60% bet $75,000 on the Spurs' victory over the Suns in the NBA regular season game.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that in the Polymarket "NBA Regular Season Spurs vs. Suns" prediction event, an account with a winning percentage over 60% (address: 0x07b8e44b90cc3e91b8d5fe60ea810d2534638e25) bet $75,000 on the Spurs defeating the Suns, with an opening price of 80.9¢. The NBA regular season game between the Spurs and Suns started at 8:00 AM Beijing time this morning and is currently in halftime. The current score is 59-52, with the Spurs trailing by 7 points, and the price is temporarily quoted at 68¢. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.description: According to Gate data, spot silver rose more than 1.00% intraday, currently trading at $73.55 per ounce.

data.title: US stocks closed lower across the board for crypto stocks, with CRCL falling 3.4%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, the crypto sector generally declined at the close of US stock markets, with MSTR down 1.65%, SBET down 2.41%, BMNR down 1.26%, and CRCL down 3.4%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: 7.0 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes South Shetland Islands
data.description: A 7.0 magnitude earthquake occurred in the South Shetland Islands, according to the United States Geological Survey's earthquake information network. The quake struck at 00:22 GMT on March 20, with a depth of 17.2 kilometers. According to Jin10, the earthquake's epicenter was located in the Antarctic region, specifically affecting the South Shetland Islands. No immediate reports of damage or casualties have been released, and further assessments are underway to determine the impact of the seismic event.

data.title: Asia Currencies Stabilize Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Asian currencies showed signs of consolidation as traders closely monitored developments in the Middle East. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the cautious approach taken by investors in response to geopolitical uncertainties. The region's currencies, including the Japanese yen and Chinese yuan, remained relatively stable despite ongoing tensions.

Market participants are assessing the potential impact of Middle East conflicts on global economic stability and trade. The situation has led to a cautious sentiment among traders, who are wary of sudden shifts in currency values. Analysts suggest that while the immediate effects on Asian currencies are limited, prolonged instability could lead to more significant market reactions.

The geopolitical landscape continues to be a focal point for investors, with attention on how these developments might influence central bank policies and economic indicators. As traders navigate these complexities, the focus remains on maintaining stability in the currency markets.

data.title: ShapeShift Founder Erik Voorhees Acquires 17,084 ETH for $36.75 Million
data.description: A wallet linked to ShapeShift founder Erik Voorhees has reportedly spent 36.75 million USDT to acquire 17,084 ETH. According to NS3.AI, the purchase price was $2,151 per ETH.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Seeks to Refocus Immigration Policy, Wall Street Journal Reports
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has reportedly expressed concerns to his inner circle about certain large-scale deportation policies, according to the Wall Street Journal. Trump has indicated a desire to concentrate more on apprehending 'bad actors' and reducing chaos in American cities. According to Jin10, this shift in focus comes amid ongoing debates over immigration enforcement and its impact on urban areas. The President's remarks suggest a potential recalibration of strategies to address crime and disorder while balancing immigration control measures.

data.title: European Union Faces Extended Energy Price Shock Due to Iran's Impact on Qatar Gas Plant
data.description: The European Union is preparing for a prolonged period of elevated energy prices following Iran's disruption of a crucial gas plant in Qatar. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential for a significant supply shortage that could last for years. This development has intensified concerns over energy security and economic stability within the region. The incident underscores the vulnerability of the EU's energy supply chain and the potential for geopolitical tensions to impact global energy markets. As the EU navigates this challenge, the focus will likely shift towards finding alternative energy sources and enhancing energy resilience.
